appraisal_events.evidence: « ...je citerai ici quelques passages comme exemple de folie unilatérale chez un homme intelligent s'adressant à un imbécile sensé. »
appraisal_events.explanation: By introducing Charlus’s letter as an example of one-sided folly, the narrator frames Charlus’s impassioned, grandiose missive to Aimé as ridiculous and misdirected, locally lowering Charlus’s standing despite his social rank.

status_effects.explanation: Charlus appears unbalanced and foolish in the narrator’s framing of his letter, reducing his local esteem despite signs of wealth and prestige.
